messages.subject: [R-jobs] S-Plus / Quant - 12 month contract job
messages.body_plain:
Position details

*         12 month contract for a leading asset management company.
Position based in London.

Experience

*         Analyst required with a background of statistical software,
ideally S-Plus, and a quantitative methods.

 

Knowledge

*         Detailed knowledge of the investment management business

*         Knowledge of S-Plus or similar statistical software

*         Ability to write SQL queries

*         Strong experience of quantitative methods.

 

Addition experience beneficial but not essential

*         Experience with fixed income technology systems and platforms

*         Knowledge of fixed income database structure

 

Qualifications

*         Would typically hold a bachelor's degree 

*         An advance degree is strongly desired, e.g. MBA or CFA
(Chartered Financial Analyst) Qualification

 

Other Personal Attributes Required

 

*         High level of numeracy and literacy

*         Strong analytical skills

*         Proven results focus

*         Clear and convincing communication style

*         Ability to articulate and defend own investment approach

*         Strong influencer

*         Self motivated to work on own initiative

*         Independent thinker

 

Contact

Please contact the managing Recruiter, Tom Carver at MDL Howarth. 

DDL: 0844 879 4570 (dialling from the UK only)

MOB: +44 (0)7916 140 978

tom.carver at mdlhowarth.com

www.mdlhowarth.com 

 

Many thanks in advance.

 

Rgds

 

Tom Carver

First Floor, Indigo House, Holbrooke Place, Richmond, TW10 6UD

DDL: 0844 879 4570 (dialling from the UK only)

MOB: +44 (0)7916 140 978

tom.carver at mdlhowarth.com

www.mdlhowarth.com 

_____________________________

This e-mail and any files transmitted with it are confid...{{dropped:28}}

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Biostatistician position, Austria
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
  My company located in Innsbruck, Austria, has an opening for a
biostatistician. If you have any interest, feel free to contact me or Dr
Markus Speiser (markus.speiser*at*biocrates.com).
 
 Best regards,

   David

##################
 Biostatistician (m/f)

Job Description

BIOCRATES Life Sciences AG is a leading metabolomics company using a
mass-spectrometry based technology platform. Its products and services
extract, deliver, and present rich information residing in metabolite
networks with unprecedented speed, low cost, and high reliability.
Biocrates? quantitative approach enables immediate identification of
metabolites, measurement of their absolute concentrations, and mapping
to their respective pathways.

We are looking for a PhD holder in Bioinformatics with a minimum of
three years working experience to support our biomarker team in the
identification, validation and further development of biomarkers. The
chosen candidate will be part of our multidisciplinary team conducting
studies to discover and develop diagnostic biomarkers, as well as
biomarkers for the characterization of disease models and drug effects,
including side effects. 

Essential functions
* Analyzing omics data and integration of Metabolomics with other omics
data- selection of likely metabolite, protein and RNA targets for
quantification. 
* Contributing to presentations, patent applications, scientific
publications and reporting on project funding.

Requirements
* Candidate can work independently and is highly motivated, creative and
result-oriented.
* Problem solving skills and strong team orientation are fundamental.
* Good command of the English language (both oral and written).
* Candidate can statistically analyze and interpret metabolomics data
and other biological data and be part of a multi disciplinary research
team that conducts metabolomics studies to discover biomarkers for the
characterization of disease models, drug and side effects.
* Candidate can control the quality of metabolomics data and other
biological data obtained from clinicals samples / animal samples and
prepare data for analysis applying various normalisation methods.
* Candidate can provide statistical input to study protocols,
statistical analysis and study plans / integrated study statistical
summaries for biomarker trials including reports; provide listings,
summary tables, and figures?patient data scientific standards for
studies.?
* Candidate can explore, test and optimise classification and
classification methods to enable the identification and refinement of
marker sets as diagnostic tools for selected disease areas.
* Candidate can improve methods and procedures to establish and ensure
the highest quality of work and results.
* Knowledge of existing bioinformatics software tools for gene, protein
and metabolite identification and expression analysis desirable;
practical experience with omics data analysis software (e.g. R package),
comparative analysis, data mining, scientific visualization and
experimental design a plus. 
* Background in life sciences strongly preferred.

#####
Contact

Dr. Markus Speiser
BIOCRATES Life Sciences AG
Innrain 66
6020 Innsbruck, Austria
Tel: +43 512 579823
email: markus.speiser*at*biocrates.com



__________________________________
David Enot 
Biomarker Research
BIOCRATES Life Sciences AG 
Innrain 66
A-6020 Innsbruck 
Austria  
Tel. +43 (512) 57 98 23 - 4221
Fax. +43 (512) 57 98 23 - 4270
www.biocrates.com 

"Creating knowledge for health"
 __________________________________

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Bioinformaticist-Biostatistician Wanted
messages.body_plain:
Job Description: 

Experimental design and analysis of large scale LC-MS based metabolomics
experiments for the identification of biomarkers, creation of predictive
models, and biological network based interpretation of the results. The
candidate should have a track record demonstrating the ability to be
self-motivated and self-directing, to develop realistic project plans
with timelines, and to solve problems using application of scientific
theory.

 

http://www.stemina.com/web/career.php

 

Please send CV to:

Dana Kelly

Office Administrator

Stemina Biomarker Discovery, Inc.

504 S. Rosa Road, Suite 150

Madison, Wisconsin 53719

608-204-0104 (telephone)

608-204-0107 (fax)

dkelly at stemina.com

 


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-jobs] R Programming Consultant Needed
messages.body_plain:
The Peabody Research Institute at Vanderbilt University is looking for a Consultant who can develop an R package for robust standard error calculations in meta-regression models. The consultant will be expected to translate an already developed R function into a more fully functional R package with additional analysis and output options. The ideal candidate will be an experienced R programmer who is available for consultation immediately.



Pay is commensurate with experience. If interested, please email Emily at e.tanner-smith at vanderbilt.edu<mailto:e.tanner-smith at vanderbilt.edu> for more information.

Thanks,
Emily Tanner-Smith
e.tanner-smith at vanderbilt.edu<mailto:e.tanner-smith at vanderbilt.edu>



	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
